stories.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care, Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen win
stories.summary: Scottish darts player Cameron Menzies received medical attention after being taken ill during his World Matchplay second-round match against Ross Smith in Blackpool on Wednesday, experiencing high blood pressure that caused him to faint. Menzies was cleared by medics but unable to return to the stage, forfeiting the match. Meanwhile, Gerwyn Price and Gian van Veen advanced to the quarter-finals, with Price winning 11-5 against Ross Cross and van Veen overcoming Wessel Nijman in a tie-break. The tournament continues with Price set to face Ross Smith in the next round.

stories.title: 'Kick Argentina out of the World Cup' petition nearly breaks Guinness World Record
stories.summary: Argentina's national team is facing a potential ban from the World Cup after a petition gathered over 23 million signatures worldwide, nearly breaking the Guinness World Record. The petition, launched during the 2026 World Cup, accused FIFA and referees of bias towards Argentina and its captain Lionel Messi. An investigation has been launched into Argentina's behavior in the final against Spain, where they were involved in altercations with the opposing team. FIFA's disciplinary committee will assess the actions of the Argentinian team.
stories.trend: fans petition fifa to replay the world cup final match

stories.title: Tierpark Hellabrunn: Münchner Zoo schläfert seine letzten Mähnenrobben ein
stories.summary: Two Mähnenrobben have been euthanized at the Tierpark Hellabrunn in Munich due to suspected Mycobacterium pinnipedii infection, which can cause tuberculosis in marine mammals. The decision was made by a commission after thorough investigations and consultations with international experts. The last two Mähnenrobben were the only ones remaining in the park, following previous cases of suspected infection. The park's water features will continue to operate normally.

stories.title: Chiffre d'affaires annuel record pour 2CRSi
stories.summary: French technology company 2CRSi has reported a record annual revenue of 416.2 million euros, a 88% increase from the previous year. This achievement validates the company's strategic shift towards artificial intelligence infrastructure two years ago. The company's net cash position stands at 14.4 million euros, its highest level since its 2018 stock market listing. 2CRSi aims to reach 1 billion euros in revenue by 2026-2027.

stories.title: William Shatner and daughter Melanie faced stage four cancer at same time
stories.summary: William Shatner, 95, and his daughter Melanie have revealed they both faced stage four cancer at the same time. Melanie was diagnosed with stage four breast cancer in 2022, while Shatner was diagnosed with stage four malignant melanoma in June 2023, which had spread to his lungs and brain. The Star Trek actor underwent surgery and two years of immunotherapy. The pair shared their health struggles in a recent interview with People magazine.

stories.title: La Cibeles entra en quirófano con visitas guiadas a su intervención
stories.summary: The iconic Fuente de Cibeles in Madrid will be partially closed for a five-month restoration starting this summer, but visitors can still get a unique perspective on the process through guided tours. The city's "Abierto por Restauración" program will offer 176 guided tours between July and September, allowing 2,640 people to see the monument's inner workings. The tours will provide a close-up look at the sculpture and its details, as well as insights into the restoration process and the monument's history.

stories.title: El Gobierno propone a Yolanda Díaz para dirigir la Organización Internacional del Trabajo
stories.summary: Spanish Vice President Yolanda Díaz has been proposed by the government to lead the International Labour Organization, a key United Nations agency promoting decent work and social justice worldwide. This would be the first time a woman from Spain heads the organization. The proposal comes after Díaz's demonstrated commitment to labor rights and social dialogue through her policies and results. The outcome of her candidacy is yet to be determined.

stories.title: Was geschah am 23. Juli?
stories.summary: Today, German football coach Julian Nagelsmann turns 39, marking a significant milestone in his career as the youngest trainer in Bundesliga history. Born in 1987, Nagelsmann has led teams such as Hoffenheim and Bayern Munich. His current tenure as Bundestrainer began in 2023 and continues through 2026. As Nagelsmann celebrates another year, his professional journey remains a notable story in German football.

stories.title: Seattle Darling Jimothy The Raccoon Has Scampered Into Hearts Across The Globe
stories.summary: A viral video of a unique-looking raccoon named Jimothy has captured the hearts of millions worldwide. The Seattle-based raccoon, known for its short neck, has been viewed over 7 million times since its July 14 release. Jimothy's charm has brought joy to fans during difficult times, with the video's creator, Kiana Hall, saying it's a welcome distraction. The raccoon's popularity is expected to continue, with fans eagerly awaiting more sightings of the beloved critter.

stories.title: "À la place de Zidane, j'ai des red flags": l'inquiétude d'Acherchour pour Rayan Cherki après sa Coupe du monde "ratée"
stories.summary: French footballer Rayan Cherki, who played for Manchester City, had a disappointing performance in the World Cup, playing only 124 minutes and starting in just one match. He was a substitute in six games, failing to make a significant impact. Cherki's frustration was evident, particularly after being substituted late in the match against Senegal. His attitude towards coach Didier Deschamps during the tournament has also been criticized.

stories.title: Tributes following sudden death of talented Meath athlete Rachel McManus
stories.summary: Rachel McManus, a talented Meath athlete, died suddenly at Dublin’s Mater Hospital on July 18. The 24-year-old played for Kilbride GFC and Dunboyne AC, with both clubs leading tributes. She was known for her warmth and was inseparable from her sister Ruby, also a club member. Funeral arrangements are pending.

stories.title: Inter, fuori un altro per la fascia: niente da fare per Spence, ecco le alternative
stories.summary: Italian football club Inter has dropped pursuit of Tottenham's Djed Spence due to a reported 47 million euro transfer fee. The club is now exploring alternative options, including 23-year-old Guela Doué of Strasburgo and 28-year-old Nahuel Molina of Atletico Madrid. Inter's director Ausilio is seeking young and strong players who can be sold at a high price in the future. The club may also consider Ivan Perisic, who could return to Inter if called upon.

stories.title: ARN Media to Present FY26 Half Year Results via Live Webinar on 21 August 2026
stories.summary: ARN Media will present its FY26 half year results on Friday 21 August 2026, covering the period to 30 June 2026. The live webinar will be hosted by CEO Michael Stephenson and CFO Alexis Poole at 9:00 am AEST. Investors can register in advance via the company's Investor Hub platform. The results will be available for review after the presentation.

stories.title: GMB presenter forced to apologise as co-star's caught yawning during serious report
stories.summary: A serious report on a tragic incident at West Mersea beach in Essex turned awkward when a Good Morning Britain reporter was caught yawning live on air. The incident occurred on July 23, 2026, as Jay Akbar failed to respond to Richard Madeley's question, instead yawning audibly. Richard Madeley was forced to apologize to the audience, attributing the issue to communication problems. The show continued with Richard attempting to move on from the awkward moment.

stories.title: Zwei Boeing 737 Max nach nur fünf Monaten im Dienst verschrottet
stories.summary: Two brand new Boeing 737 Max 8 aircraft, delivered to Gol Linhas Aéreas in Brazil in September and November last year, were scrapped just five months later after being removed from service due to chronic problems. The planes, with registration numbers PS-GRM and PS-GRN, were flown to the Pinal Airpark in Arizona, a major aircraft graveyard, on March 29. The airline reportedly returned the jets to their leasing company due to frequent unavailability.

stories.title: RB Leipzig: Ex-Bundesliga-Star Kampl ist jetzt Gärtner auf Mallorca
stories.summary: Former RB Leipzig midfielder Kevin Kampl, 35, has transitioned to a life as a gardener on the island of Mallorca, where he grows his own produce including melons, tomatoes, and olives. Kampl's decision comes after a difficult year, which included the loss of his brother Seki and his father. He has been cultivating his garden for years, even during his active football career. Kampl's family, including his wife and two sons, have joined him on the island, where they are now enjoying a quieter life.

stories.title: Desapareció en el País Vasco francés en 2018 sin dejar rastro, hasta hoy: buzos estadounidenses encuentran el coche de David Stévenin
stories.summary: A nearly eight-year-old mystery in the French Basque region has been reopened after a team of American divers found the car of David Stévenin, a 29-year-old man who disappeared in 2018. The Renault Mégane was located at the bottom of the Adour river, where human remains were found inside. The investigation has been reactivated to determine if the remains belong to Stévenin and to shed light on his disappearance on September 26, 2018, in the town of Urt. The discovery brings closure to a family that had never given up hope of finding answers.

stories.title: 'Sauer in trek: Champions League-club meldt zich bij Feyenoord, vraagprijs bekend'
stories.summary: Feyenoord's Leo Sauer is being pursued by two German clubs, VfB Stuttgart and TSG Hoffenheim, with the latter having already reached a contract agreement with the 20-year-old player. The transfer fee reportedly sought by Feyenoord is over 15 million euros, significantly higher than the 7 million euros mentioned. Stuttgart is currently exploring other attacking options before considering Sauer.

stories.title: STMicroelectronics prevede ricavi superiori a 1 miliardo di dollari nel 2026 grazie a data center per l'IA
stories.summary: STMicroelectronics, a global semiconductor leader, has reported net revenues of 3.49 billion dollars in the second quarter of 2026, a 26% increase from the same period last year. The company's net income reached 222 million dollars, exceeding analyst expectations. This growth is driven by increased demand in the Communication Equipment and Computer Peripherals and Automotive sectors. The company now expects revenues to exceed 1 billion dollars in 2026.

stories.title: What happened to Ryan Gosling’s ‘La La Hand’ on the La La Land poster?
stories.summary: Ryan Gosling's "La La Hand" pose in the La La Land poster has been altered to its original form, marking the 10th anniversary of the film. The change corrects a pose where Gosling's wrist was bent, a detail he himself disliked. According to Gosling, he intentionally flattened his hand during filming, but it became a defining image for the movie. The new poster is a nod to Gosling's original intention, as revealed in a 2024 interview with the Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Mattarella compie 85 anni: le sfide al Colle tra elezioni e Costituzione
stories.summary: Italian President Sergio Mattarella turns 85 today, marking the 11th year of his tenure amidst various political crises and elections. The upcoming elections pose a challenge, with Mattarella potentially needing to dissolve the Chambers early or allow the current legislature to reach its natural end in September. The President will face a contentious campaign season, with the fate of the electoral law still uncertain.

stories.title: A Castellanza due chilometri di nuova condotta per potenziare la rete dell’acqua
stories.summary: A major water infrastructure project is underway in Castellanza, Italy, where CAP Holding is installing approximately two kilometers of new water pipeline to connect the city's east and west sides. The project aims to improve water efficiency and reliability over the next decades. The work is expected to last around a year and will affect several city streets. The new pipeline will be completed in phases, with the first stage starting near Corso Sempione.

stories.title: DIRECT. Incendie en Gironde : 2 000 hectares brûlés entre Saumos et Le Porge, plus de 10 000 nouvelles évacuations dans la nuit, 500 pompiers mobilisés... Suivez les dernières infos ce jeudi
stories.summary: A massive wildfire in southwestern France has burned over 2,000 hectares of land between Saumos and Le Porge, forcing more than 10,000 new evacuations overnight. Approximately 500 firefighters have been deployed to combat the blaze. The fire is still raging, with no containment in sight. Authorities are urging residents to remain vigilant and follow evacuation orders.

stories.title: FC Bayern: Jérôme Boateng kündigt Michael Olise den Mietvertrag für Luxus-Villa!
stories.summary: German footballer Michael Olise, 24, has been evicted from his luxury villa in Grünwald, a Munich suburb, after Bayern star Jérôme Boateng, 37, terminated their rental agreement. The decision affects Olise's living arrangements, with a specific move-out date not specified. Boateng, a 2014 World Cup winner, is the villa's owner. The exact circumstances behind the decision remain unclear.

stories.title: Tour de France 2026 : le parcours, le profil et les horaires de la 18e étape entre Voiron et Orcières-Merlette
stories.summary: The 18th stage of the 2026 Tour de France will cover 185.2 kilometers from Voiron to Orcières-Merlette on July 23, with a 5% chance of rain. The stage features three difficult climbs, including the 7.1 kilometer ascent to Orcières-Merlette with a 6.7% gradient. This stage is expected to favor climbers and breakaway riders, with 3,800 meters of elevation gain. The stage is set to conclude at 5:25 PM local time.

stories.title: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar 'delighted' to accept trade minister job
stories.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar will step down to become UK Minister of State for Trade, leaving his party role after three years. He will join the House of Lords via a peerage to take the position. Sarwar cited public service as his motivation, despite past criticism of the Lords. Scottish Labour suffered its worst election result in May, winning just 17 of 129 seats.

stories.title: Charity 'heartbroken' as food ruined in break-in
stories.summary: Charity FareShare North East lost 16 tonnes of food—enough for 7,000 meals—after thieves cut power to its Middlesbrough warehouse on Tuesday, ruining perishables. The break-in forced staff to discard milk, meat and other supplies at the charity’s Teesside base. Middlesbrough hub manager Natasha Flanagan called the damage heartbreaking, noting ransacked lockers and open freezer doors. The charity, which redistributes supermarket donations to over 200 groups, plans to assess the full impact and continue operations.

stories.title: Third person dies from NYC Legionnaires’ outbreak
stories.summary: A third person has died from the Legionnaires' disease outbreak on New York City's Upper East Side, bringing the total number of deaths to three. At least 74 cases have been reported with 59 people hospitalized, and eight remain in the hospital. Health officials believe the source of the outbreak has likely been eliminated, citing a recent decline in new cases. Further investigation is needed to confirm the source of the outbreak.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google’s parent company Alphabet reported negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion in its latest quarter, the first time in over a decade, as AI infrastructure costs surged to $45 billion in Q2 alone. Total AI spending is projected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion, driven by server and data center investments. Despite revenue hitting $119.8 billion, a 23% year-over-year increase, Alphabet’s stock dropped 4% in after-hours trading. The company’s CFO attributed the cash burn to capital expenditures tied entirely to AI expansion.

stories.title: HNK Rijeka tie 'a great opportunity' for Derry
stories.summary: Derry City face HNK Rijeka in the second round of the UEFA Conference League qualifiers on August 1. The tie follows Derry’s 5-3 aggregate loss to CSKA Sofia in the Europa League opener, where crowd trouble at Brandywell led to a temporary match stoppage and police intervention. As a result, Derry received a suspended partial stadium ban for two years and a €20,000 fine. Lynch’s side aims to recover from the setback with a strong home performance.

stories.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
stories.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK’s 20 busiest airports have jumped by a third on average since last summer, with Gatwick charging £10 for 10 minutes and Stansted £10 for 15 minutes. The RAC found the average fee rose to £7, up £1.70, while London City Airport’s £8 for five minutes makes it the priciest per minute. Airports UK says free drop-offs remain available, framing fees as part of business and climate strategies. The trend contrasts with Europe’s top 10 airports, where none charge for drop-offs.

stories.title: Sorteo de Oro de la Cruz Roja 2026: a qué hora es hoy, dónde ver online, premios y cómo cobrarlos
stories.summary: Tonight, the 47th edition of the Sorteo de Oro de la Cruz Roja 2026 will award over 7 million euros in 23,400 prizes, with the top prize of 3 million euros. The drawing will take place on Thursday, July 23, with tickets available for purchase until 3:00 PM. Winners can claim their prizes in cash or equivalent gold. The drawing's increased prize pool is a significant milestone for the humanitarian institution's most solidary lottery.

stories.title: Weekly sports quiz: Glasgow 2026, The Open and The Hundred
stories.summary: Glasgow will host the 2026 Commonwealth Games, with preparations underway as record transfer spending shakes up football and The Hundred cricket tournament kicks off this summer. Last week’s sports quiz saw only 6% of participants score full marks. The event follows a busy week in sports, including the World Cup final build-up. Stay updated on quizzes and sports news via the dedicated pages.

stories.title: Ex-UFC champion Andrade working as delivery driver
stories.summary: Jessica Andrade, former UFC strawweight champion, is working as a delivery driver in Brazil to earn extra income while recovering from a shoulder injury. The 34-year-old, who hasn’t competed since August 2023, has fought 30 times in the UFC since 2013. She also started a landscaping project at a friend’s house to supplement her earnings. Andrade plans to return to fighting once fully recovered.

stories.title: Business rates to be cut by 20% for pubs, clubs and music venues in England
stories.summary: A 20% cut to business rates for pubs, clubs and music venues in England will take effect from April, saving nearly 32,000 businesses an average of £1,100 each. The £100m package, announced by Andy Burnham, aims to ease cost pressures on local high streets. Funding will come from reviewing reliefs for businesses that do not benefit communities. The move follows other cost of living measures, including capped bus fares and reduced VAT on electricity bills.

stories.title: River levels approach all-time low as drought announcement looms
stories.summary: River levels in Wales are nearing record lows as drought conditions intensify. Natural Resources Wales is set to declare an environmental drought for parts of the country on Thursday, following a July with just 5% of average rainfall. The dry spell, worsened by back-to-back heatwaves, has devastated wildlife and threatened crops and livestock. Farmers and conservation groups warn of worsening impacts if conditions persist.

stories.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
stories.summary: Indian climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, on a 26-day hunger strike protesting government policies, was admitted to Medanta Hospital’s ICU in Gurugram on Wednesday after losing 11kg. Doctors report he remains stable and conscious, receiving treatment with his consent. Opposition leaders and health officials have urged him to end the fast, but Wangchuk has refused. The government says it remains open to discussions on his demands.

stories.title: Streeting says Burnham government will prioritise military over other spending
stories.summary: UK Defence Secretary Wes Streeting has reaffirmed the government's commitment to increase defence spending to 3.5% of GDP by 2035, a target that would require a real-terms increase of over £25 billion in less than a decade. This move prioritizes military spending over other departments. The increased spending is expected to contribute to economic growth, but will come at an opportunity cost across government. The UK's defence budget is set to rise significantly in the coming years.

stories.title: « Nous avons davantage de visibilité sur la demande autour de l'IA » : Soitec surprend le marché en relevant nettement ses prévisions avec une semaine d'avance
stories.summary: French semi-conductor company Soitec has released its first quarter results a week ahead of schedule, surprising the market with significantly revised upward projections. The company cites increased visibility into demand for artificial intelligence and photonics due to recent actions and client commitments. Soitec's results were initially expected next week. This move is attributed to a desire for transparency and rigor.

stories.title: Trainspotting the Musical review – forgettable songs make this a bad trip
stories.summary: A musical adaptation of Irvine Welsh's novel Trainspotting opened in London, but critics say it fails to capture the energy of the original film. The show, written by Welsh and directed by Caroline Jay Ranger, features a disjointed storyline and forgettable songs. The production, which includes a cast of young actors, has been panned for its lackluster portrayal of heroin addiction. The musical's failure to deliver a compelling story may leave audiences disappointed.

stories.title: The millennial generation has split, new Fed research shows: those over 35 are edging toward boomer-style wealth, while everyone else falls behind
stories.summary: According to new Federal Reserve research, the US homeownership rate has a significant age-based split, with those over 35 edging toward boomer-style wealth, while those under 35 fall behind. The actual homeownership rate is around 53%, not the reported 65%, with just 22% of adults under 35 owning their homes. This gap is attributed to the traditional rate only counting household heads, not all adults in the age group. The consequences of this disparity are yet to be seen.

stories.title: Mercedes-Benz SLK R170 - La roadster che reinventò il tetto rigido
stories.summary: A roadster revolutionized by a rigid metal roof, the Mercedes-Benz SLK R170, debuted 30 years ago in 1996. The car's innovative design featured a compact size, rear-wheel drive, and a retractable metal roof that could disappear into the trunk in about 25 seconds. Produced from 1996 to 2004, the SLK R170 was a game-changer in the automotive industry, paving the way for future convertible models. Its production continued for nearly a decade.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukraine’s drone strikes on Russia’s largest online retailer Wildberries have destroyed multiple warehouses in recent days, killing eight workers and disrupting supplies for thousands of small businesses. The attacks in Elektrostal and Kotovsk, 400km from the border, follow Kyiv’s shift to targeting logistics linked to military procurement. Hundreds of Russian sellers report losses exceeding £750m, with livelihoods at risk as the war’s impact spreads closer to home.

stories.title: GMB's Andi Peters announces departure of beloved colleague
stories.summary: Good Morning Britain series director Stuart Earl is leaving the show after 10 years. He will be departing this week. Earl has worked on the program since its inception. The news was announced live on air by presenter Andi Peters, who joked about having to work with Earl for the next two days.

stories.title: Butterfly city blooms in shadow of office blocks
stories.summary: A mini meadow in Hull city centre has attracted ten butterfly species, including the rare small skipper, thriving amid office blocks and a shopping centre. The 2023 project, led by Hull Butterfly City, allows grass and wildflowers to grow on a Land Registry site near St Stephen’s Shopping Centre to support caterpillars. Sean Clough noted the small skipper’s winter survival depends on uncut grass. The scheme aims to boost urban wildlife by providing specific host plants for caterpillars.

stories.title: Ann Widdecombe public memorial to be held later this year, family says
stories.summary: A public memorial for former Conservative minister and Reform UK spokeswoman Ann Widdecombe will be held later this year, her family announced. Widdecombe, 78, was found dead in her Dartmoor home earlier this month with serious head injuries. A private service for close family and friends will take place in the coming weeks, with invitations already sent. Tributes have poured in from across the political spectrum and TV personalities.

stories.title: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
stories.summary: Animal lovers in China are escalating their campaign against animal cruelty after a viral video showed the killing of a stray dog and her puppies, despite government censorship. Authorities cited the young age of the four boys involved as they urged online silence, but public outrage surged, pushing activists to take protests offline and overseas, including billboards in New York. Three weeks later, social media posts about the incident still draw thousands of comments, reflecting sustained anger and sorrow. The movement shows no signs of slowing as activists seek broader support beyond China’s censored platforms.

stories.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
stories.summary: ServiceNow has invested $40 million in Indian banking software specialist BusinessNext, valuing the 24-year-old firm at $700 million and taking a roughly 5% stake. This deal gives BusinessNext access to ServiceNow's global sales network as they expand their partnership in AI for financial services. BusinessNext serves over 70 banks across India, Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and the US, with half of its revenue coming from outside India. The partnership is expected to drive growth for BusinessNext in overseas markets.

stories.title: Global Oil Prices Rise as Conflict With Iran Deepens
stories.summary: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
stories.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

stories.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
stories.summary: The Trump administration announced an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

stories.title: Alarm over hospital lung admissions for children in England’s poorest postcodes
stories.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalized for respiratory infections than those in wealthier regions, new NHS data shows. In 2024/25, 1,941 per 100,000 children in the most deprived areas were admitted, compared to 1,380 per 100,000 in the least deprived. Experts link the disparity to factors like air pollution, though the data doesn’t specify causes. Health officials plan further research to address the growing concern.

stories.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
stories.summary: A stunning NASA image from July 23, 2026 captures the rugged Olympic Mountains in Washington state, showcasing snow-capped peaks and deep river valleys. The astronaut-taken panorama highlights the peninsula’s mix of alpine glaciers, temperate rainforests, and coastal wilderness. Olympic National Park covers much of the mountainous core, with surrounding areas managed as national forest, state land, or tribal territory. The mountains’ basalt rock formed 55 to 15 million years ago from undersea volcanic activity.

stories.title: Fatboy Slim helps to save 'lifeblood' music venue
stories.summary: A music venue charity has bought its tenth UK club to protect grassroots music, making it the country’s largest dedicated landlord of live music spaces. The Pipeline in Brighton was saved from closure after years of financial pressure, securing 6,000 jobs and reversing a 13% rise in grassroots gig attendance lost last year. The charity’s model buys venues to keep rents affordable for artists and communities. Next, it plans to expand its network to prevent further closures.

stories.title: Courtney Stodden Is Fighting for Something Bigger Than Jason Alexander’s Apology
stories.summary: In 2012, Jason Alexander apologized to Courtney Stodden for a 2012 "Dr. Phil" parody sketch where he made inappropriate comments and simulated fondling her when she was 17. The sketch, part of "The Donny Clay Show," was removed from Funny or Die and YouTube. Stodden, who married actor Doug Hutchison at 16, is now seeking accountability after years of public scrutiny. Alexander’s apology follows years of criticism over the sketch’s exploitation of a minor.

stories.title: ‘The Wild Robot’ VFX Studio STIM Sets Canary Islands Operation, Planning to Create More Than 100 Jobs
stories.summary: French animation and VFX studio STIM is launching a new production center in Spain’s Canary Islands, aiming to create over 100 jobs by 2026. Operations at STIM Tenerife begin in August, with 60 roles already open for skilled artists and supervisors. The studio chose the Canary Islands for its low-tax ZEC regime, tapping into a growing creative talent pool. This expansion will boost STIM’s production capacity while maintaining its high artistic and technical standards.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine has been created, priced at $200, featuring a basic fully functional game with a working joystick and button to control Mario's movement and jumping. The machine includes an endless series of barrels for Mario to jump, utilizing the same original barrels first released in 1989 for Lego's pirate sets. Shigeru Miyamoto, creator of Mario, has reportedly expressed approval for the set. The machine's design is a nostalgic tribute to classic arcade games.

stories.title: Life-changing MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
stories.summary: A new NHS England scheme will provide the MS drug fampridine to up to 5,000 patients annually, improving walking ability for those with multiple sclerosis. The treatment, which boosts nerve signals, showed mobility gains in 40% of trial participants. Previously costly, it was already available in other UK regions. Patients will now access it alongside existing NHS support like physiotherapy.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care, Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen win
offbeat.summary: Scottish darts player Cameron Menzies received medical attention after being taken ill during his World Matchplay second-round match against Ross Smith in Blackpool on Wednesday, experiencing high blood pressure that caused him to faint. Menzies was cleared by medics but unable to return to the stage, forfeiting the match. Meanwhile, Gerwyn Price and Gian van Veen advanced to the quarter-finals, with Price winning 11-5 against Ross Cross and van Veen overcoming Wessel Nijman in a tie-break. The tournament continues with Price set to face Ross Smith in the next round.

offbeat.title: 'Kick Argentina out of the World Cup' petition nearly breaks Guinness World Record
offbeat.summary: Argentina's national team is facing a potential ban from the World Cup after a petition gathered over 23 million signatures worldwide, nearly breaking the Guinness World Record. The petition, launched during the 2026 World Cup, accused FIFA and referees of bias towards Argentina and its captain Lionel Messi. An investigation has been launched into Argentina's behavior in the final against Spain, where they were involved in altercations with the opposing team. FIFA's disciplinary committee will assess the actions of the Argentinian team.
offbeat.trend: fans petition fifa to replay the world cup final match

offbeat.title: Tierpark Hellabrunn: Münchner Zoo schläfert seine letzten Mähnenrobben ein
offbeat.summary: Two Mähnenrobben have been euthanized at the Tierpark Hellabrunn in Munich due to suspected Mycobacterium pinnipedii infection, which can cause tuberculosis in marine mammals. The decision was made by a commission after thorough investigations and consultations with international experts. The last two Mähnenrobben were the only ones remaining in the park, following previous cases of suspected infection. The park's water features will continue to operate normally.

offbeat.title: Chiffre d'affaires annuel record pour 2CRSi
offbeat.summary: French technology company 2CRSi has reported a record annual revenue of 416.2 million euros, a 88% increase from the previous year. This achievement validates the company's strategic shift towards artificial intelligence infrastructure two years ago. The company's net cash position stands at 14.4 million euros, its highest level since its 2018 stock market listing. 2CRSi aims to reach 1 billion euros in revenue by 2026-2027.

offbeat.title: William Shatner and daughter Melanie faced stage four cancer at same time
offbeat.summary: William Shatner, 95, and his daughter Melanie have revealed they both faced stage four cancer at the same time. Melanie was diagnosed with stage four breast cancer in 2022, while Shatner was diagnosed with stage four malignant melanoma in June 2023, which had spread to his lungs and brain. The Star Trek actor underwent surgery and two years of immunotherapy. The pair shared their health struggles in a recent interview with People magazine.

offbeat.title: La Cibeles entra en quirófano con visitas guiadas a su intervención
offbeat.summary: The iconic Fuente de Cibeles in Madrid will be partially closed for a five-month restoration starting this summer, but visitors can still get a unique perspective on the process through guided tours. The city's "Abierto por Restauración" program will offer 176 guided tours between July and September, allowing 2,640 people to see the monument's inner workings. The tours will provide a close-up look at the sculpture and its details, as well as insights into the restoration process and the monument's history.

offbeat.title: El Gobierno propone a Yolanda Díaz para dirigir la Organización Internacional del Trabajo
offbeat.summary: Spanish Vice President Yolanda Díaz has been proposed by the government to lead the International Labour Organization, a key United Nations agency promoting decent work and social justice worldwide. This would be the first time a woman from Spain heads the organization. The proposal comes after Díaz's demonstrated commitment to labor rights and social dialogue through her policies and results. The outcome of her candidacy is yet to be determined.

offbeat.title: Was geschah am 23. Juli?
offbeat.summary: Today, German football coach Julian Nagelsmann turns 39, marking a significant milestone in his career as the youngest trainer in Bundesliga history. Born in 1987, Nagelsmann has led teams such as Hoffenheim and Bayern Munich. His current tenure as Bundestrainer began in 2023 and continues through 2026. As Nagelsmann celebrates another year, his professional journey remains a notable story in German football.

offbeat.title: Seattle Darling Jimothy The Raccoon Has Scampered Into Hearts Across The Globe
offbeat.summary: A viral video of a unique-looking raccoon named Jimothy has captured the hearts of millions worldwide. The Seattle-based raccoon, known for its short neck, has been viewed over 7 million times since its July 14 release. Jimothy's charm has brought joy to fans during difficult times, with the video's creator, Kiana Hall, saying it's a welcome distraction. The raccoon's popularity is expected to continue, with fans eagerly awaiting more sightings of the beloved critter.

offbeat.title: "À la place de Zidane, j'ai des red flags": l'inquiétude d'Acherchour pour Rayan Cherki après sa Coupe du monde "ratée"
offbeat.summary: French footballer Rayan Cherki, who played for Manchester City, had a disappointing performance in the World Cup, playing only 124 minutes and starting in just one match. He was a substitute in six games, failing to make a significant impact. Cherki's frustration was evident, particularly after being substituted late in the match against Senegal. His attitude towards coach Didier Deschamps during the tournament has also been criticized.

offbeat.title: Tributes following sudden death of talented Meath athlete Rachel McManus
offbeat.summary: Rachel McManus, a talented Meath athlete, died suddenly at Dublin’s Mater Hospital on July 18. The 24-year-old played for Kilbride GFC and Dunboyne AC, with both clubs leading tributes. She was known for her warmth and was inseparable from her sister Ruby, also a club member. Funeral arrangements are pending.

offbeat.title: Inter, fuori un altro per la fascia: niente da fare per Spence, ecco le alternative
offbeat.summary: Italian football club Inter has dropped pursuit of Tottenham's Djed Spence due to a reported 47 million euro transfer fee. The club is now exploring alternative options, including 23-year-old Guela Doué of Strasburgo and 28-year-old Nahuel Molina of Atletico Madrid. Inter's director Ausilio is seeking young and strong players who can be sold at a high price in the future. The club may also consider Ivan Perisic, who could return to Inter if called upon.

offbeat.title: ARN Media to Present FY26 Half Year Results via Live Webinar on 21 August 2026
offbeat.summary: ARN Media will present its FY26 half year results on Friday 21 August 2026, covering the period to 30 June 2026. The live webinar will be hosted by CEO Michael Stephenson and CFO Alexis Poole at 9:00 am AEST. Investors can register in advance via the company's Investor Hub platform. The results will be available for review after the presentation.

offbeat.title: GMB presenter forced to apologise as co-star's caught yawning during serious report
offbeat.summary: A serious report on a tragic incident at West Mersea beach in Essex turned awkward when a Good Morning Britain reporter was caught yawning live on air. The incident occurred on July 23, 2026, as Jay Akbar failed to respond to Richard Madeley's question, instead yawning audibly. Richard Madeley was forced to apologize to the audience, attributing the issue to communication problems. The show continued with Richard attempting to move on from the awkward moment.

offbeat.title: Zwei Boeing 737 Max nach nur fünf Monaten im Dienst verschrottet
offbeat.summary: Two brand new Boeing 737 Max 8 aircraft, delivered to Gol Linhas Aéreas in Brazil in September and November last year, were scrapped just five months later after being removed from service due to chronic problems. The planes, with registration numbers PS-GRM and PS-GRN, were flown to the Pinal Airpark in Arizona, a major aircraft graveyard, on March 29. The airline reportedly returned the jets to their leasing company due to frequent unavailability.

offbeat.title: RB Leipzig: Ex-Bundesliga-Star Kampl ist jetzt Gärtner auf Mallorca
offbeat.summary: Former RB Leipzig midfielder Kevin Kampl, 35, has transitioned to a life as a gardener on the island of Mallorca, where he grows his own produce including melons, tomatoes, and olives. Kampl's decision comes after a difficult year, which included the loss of his brother Seki and his father. He has been cultivating his garden for years, even during his active football career. Kampl's family, including his wife and two sons, have joined him on the island, where they are now enjoying a quieter life.

offbeat.title: Desapareció en el País Vasco francés en 2018 sin dejar rastro, hasta hoy: buzos estadounidenses encuentran el coche de David Stévenin
offbeat.summary: A nearly eight-year-old mystery in the French Basque region has been reopened after a team of American divers found the car of David Stévenin, a 29-year-old man who disappeared in 2018. The Renault Mégane was located at the bottom of the Adour river, where human remains were found inside. The investigation has been reactivated to determine if the remains belong to Stévenin and to shed light on his disappearance on September 26, 2018, in the town of Urt. The discovery brings closure to a family that had never given up hope of finding answers.

offbeat.title: 'Sauer in trek: Champions League-club meldt zich bij Feyenoord, vraagprijs bekend'
offbeat.summary: Feyenoord's Leo Sauer is being pursued by two German clubs, VfB Stuttgart and TSG Hoffenheim, with the latter having already reached a contract agreement with the 20-year-old player. The transfer fee reportedly sought by Feyenoord is over 15 million euros, significantly higher than the 7 million euros mentioned. Stuttgart is currently exploring other attacking options before considering Sauer.

offbeat.title: STMicroelectronics prevede ricavi superiori a 1 miliardo di dollari nel 2026 grazie a data center per l'IA
offbeat.summary: STMicroelectronics, a global semiconductor leader, has reported net revenues of 3.49 billion dollars in the second quarter of 2026, a 26% increase from the same period last year. The company's net income reached 222 million dollars, exceeding analyst expectations. This growth is driven by increased demand in the Communication Equipment and Computer Peripherals and Automotive sectors. The company now expects revenues to exceed 1 billion dollars in 2026.

offbeat.title: What happened to Ryan Gosling’s ‘La La Hand’ on the La La Land poster?
offbeat.summary: Ryan Gosling's "La La Hand" pose in the La La Land poster has been altered to its original form, marking the 10th anniversary of the film. The change corrects a pose where Gosling's wrist was bent, a detail he himself disliked. According to Gosling, he intentionally flattened his hand during filming, but it became a defining image for the movie. The new poster is a nod to Gosling's original intention, as revealed in a 2024 interview with the Wall Street Journal.

offbeat.title: Mattarella compie 85 anni: le sfide al Colle tra elezioni e Costituzione
offbeat.summary: Italian President Sergio Mattarella turns 85 today, marking the 11th year of his tenure amidst various political crises and elections. The upcoming elections pose a challenge, with Mattarella potentially needing to dissolve the Chambers early or allow the current legislature to reach its natural end in September. The President will face a contentious campaign season, with the fate of the electoral law still uncertain.

offbeat.title: A Castellanza due chilometri di nuova condotta per potenziare la rete dell’acqua
offbeat.summary: A major water infrastructure project is underway in Castellanza, Italy, where CAP Holding is installing approximately two kilometers of new water pipeline to connect the city's east and west sides. The project aims to improve water efficiency and reliability over the next decades. The work is expected to last around a year and will affect several city streets. The new pipeline will be completed in phases, with the first stage starting near Corso Sempione.

offbeat.title: DIRECT. Incendie en Gironde : 2 000 hectares brûlés entre Saumos et Le Porge, plus de 10 000 nouvelles évacuations dans la nuit, 500 pompiers mobilisés... Suivez les dernières infos ce jeudi
offbeat.summary: A massive wildfire in southwestern France has burned over 2,000 hectares of land between Saumos and Le Porge, forcing more than 10,000 new evacuations overnight. Approximately 500 firefighters have been deployed to combat the blaze. The fire is still raging, with no containment in sight. Authorities are urging residents to remain vigilant and follow evacuation orders.

offbeat.title: FC Bayern: Jérôme Boateng kündigt Michael Olise den Mietvertrag für Luxus-Villa!
offbeat.summary: German footballer Michael Olise, 24, has been evicted from his luxury villa in Grünwald, a Munich suburb, after Bayern star Jérôme Boateng, 37, terminated their rental agreement. The decision affects Olise's living arrangements, with a specific move-out date not specified. Boateng, a 2014 World Cup winner, is the villa's owner. The exact circumstances behind the decision remain unclear.

offbeat.title: Tour de France 2026 : le parcours, le profil et les horaires de la 18e étape entre Voiron et Orcières-Merlette
offbeat.summary: The 18th stage of the 2026 Tour de France will cover 185.2 kilometers from Voiron to Orcières-Merlette on July 23, with a 5% chance of rain. The stage features three difficult climbs, including the 7.1 kilometer ascent to Orcières-Merlette with a 6.7% gradient. This stage is expected to favor climbers and breakaway riders, with 3,800 meters of elevation gain. The stage is set to conclude at 5:25 PM local time.

offbeat.title: Third person dies from NYC Legionnaires’ outbreak
offbeat.summary: A third person has died from the Legionnaires' disease outbreak on New York City's Upper East Side, bringing the total number of deaths to three. At least 74 cases have been reported with 59 people hospitalized, and eight remain in the hospital. Health officials believe the source of the outbreak has likely been eliminated, citing a recent decline in new cases. Further investigation is needed to confirm the source of the outbreak.

offbeat.title: Sorteo de Oro de la Cruz Roja 2026: a qué hora es hoy, dónde ver online, premios y cómo cobrarlos
offbeat.summary: Tonight, the 47th edition of the Sorteo de Oro de la Cruz Roja 2026 will award over 7 million euros in 23,400 prizes, with the top prize of 3 million euros. The drawing will take place on Thursday, July 23, with tickets available for purchase until 3:00 PM. Winners can claim their prizes in cash or equivalent gold. The drawing's increased prize pool is a significant milestone for the humanitarian institution's most solidary lottery.

offbeat.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
offbeat.summary: Indian climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, on a 26-day hunger strike protesting government policies, was admitted to Medanta Hospital’s ICU in Gurugram on Wednesday after losing 11kg. Doctors report he remains stable and conscious, receiving treatment with his consent. Opposition leaders and health officials have urged him to end the fast, but Wangchuk has refused. The government says it remains open to discussions on his demands.

offbeat.title: Streeting says Burnham government will prioritise military over other spending
offbeat.summary: UK Defence Secretary Wes Streeting has reaffirmed the government's commitment to increase defence spending to 3.5% of GDP by 2035, a target that would require a real-terms increase of over £25 billion in less than a decade. This move prioritizes military spending over other departments. The increased spending is expected to contribute to economic growth, but will come at an opportunity cost across government. The UK's defence budget is set to rise significantly in the coming years.

offbeat.title: « Nous avons davantage de visibilité sur la demande autour de l'IA » : Soitec surprend le marché en relevant nettement ses prévisions avec une semaine d'avance
offbeat.summary: French semi-conductor company Soitec has released its first quarter results a week ahead of schedule, surprising the market with significantly revised upward projections. The company cites increased visibility into demand for artificial intelligence and photonics due to recent actions and client commitments. Soitec's results were initially expected next week. This move is attributed to a desire for transparency and rigor.

offbeat.title: Trainspotting the Musical review – forgettable songs make this a bad trip
offbeat.summary: A musical adaptation of Irvine Welsh's novel Trainspotting opened in London, but critics say it fails to capture the energy of the original film. The show, written by Welsh and directed by Caroline Jay Ranger, features a disjointed storyline and forgettable songs. The production, which includes a cast of young actors, has been panned for its lackluster portrayal of heroin addiction. The musical's failure to deliver a compelling story may leave audiences disappointed.

offbeat.title: The millennial generation has split, new Fed research shows: those over 35 are edging toward boomer-style wealth, while everyone else falls behind
offbeat.summary: According to new Federal Reserve research, the US homeownership rate has a significant age-based split, with those over 35 edging toward boomer-style wealth, while those under 35 fall behind. The actual homeownership rate is around 53%, not the reported 65%, with just 22% of adults under 35 owning their homes. This gap is attributed to the traditional rate only counting household heads, not all adults in the age group. The consequences of this disparity are yet to be seen.

offbeat.title: Mercedes-Benz SLK R170 - La roadster che reinventò il tetto rigido
offbeat.summary: A roadster revolutionized by a rigid metal roof, the Mercedes-Benz SLK R170, debuted 30 years ago in 1996. The car's innovative design featured a compact size, rear-wheel drive, and a retractable metal roof that could disappear into the trunk in about 25 seconds. Produced from 1996 to 2004, the SLK R170 was a game-changer in the automotive industry, paving the way for future convertible models. Its production continued for nearly a decade.

offbeat.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
offbeat.summary: Ukraine’s drone strikes on Russia’s largest online retailer Wildberries have destroyed multiple warehouses in recent days, killing eight workers and disrupting supplies for thousands of small businesses. The attacks in Elektrostal and Kotovsk, 400km from the border, follow Kyiv’s shift to targeting logistics linked to military procurement. Hundreds of Russian sellers report losses exceeding £750m, with livelihoods at risk as the war’s impact spreads closer to home.

offbeat.title: GMB's Andi Peters announces departure of beloved colleague
offbeat.summary: Good Morning Britain series director Stuart Earl is leaving the show after 10 years. He will be departing this week. Earl has worked on the program since its inception. The news was announced live on air by presenter Andi Peters, who joked about having to work with Earl for the next two days.

offbeat.title: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
offbeat.summary: Animal lovers in China are escalating their campaign against animal cruelty after a viral video showed the killing of a stray dog and her puppies, despite government censorship. Authorities cited the young age of the four boys involved as they urged online silence, but public outrage surged, pushing activists to take protests offline and overseas, including billboards in New York. Three weeks later, social media posts about the incident still draw thousands of comments, reflecting sustained anger and sorrow. The movement shows no signs of slowing as activists seek broader support beyond China’s censored platforms.

politics.title: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar 'delighted' to accept trade minister job
politics.summary: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar will step down to become UK Minister of State for Trade, leaving his party role after three years. He will join the House of Lords via a peerage to take the position. Sarwar cited public service as his motivation, despite past criticism of the Lords. Scottish Labour suffered its worst election result in May, winning just 17 of 129 seats.

politics.title: Business rates to be cut by 20% for pubs, clubs and music venues in England
politics.summary: A 20% cut to business rates for pubs, clubs and music venues in England will take effect from April, saving nearly 32,000 businesses an average of £1,100 each. The £100m package, announced by Andy Burnham, aims to ease cost pressures on local high streets. Funding will come from reviewing reliefs for businesses that do not benefit communities. The move follows other cost of living measures, including capped bus fares and reduced VAT on electricity bills.

politics.title: Ann Widdecombe public memorial to be held later this year, family says
politics.summary: A public memorial for former Conservative minister and Reform UK spokeswoman Ann Widdecombe will be held later this year, her family announced. Widdecombe, 78, was found dead in her Dartmoor home earlier this month with serious head injuries. A private service for close family and friends will take place in the coming weeks, with invitations already sent. Tributes have poured in from across the political spectrum and TV personalities.

crisis.title: Charity 'heartbroken' as food ruined in break-in
crisis.summary: Charity FareShare North East lost 16 tonnes of food—enough for 7,000 meals—after thieves cut power to its Middlesbrough warehouse on Tuesday, ruining perishables. The break-in forced staff to discard milk, meat and other supplies at the charity’s Teesside base. Middlesbrough hub manager Natasha Flanagan called the damage heartbreaking, noting ransacked lockers and open freezer doors. The charity, which redistributes supermarket donations to over 200 groups, plans to assess the full impact and continue operations.

crisis.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
crisis.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK’s 20 busiest airports have jumped by a third on average since last summer, with Gatwick charging £10 for 10 minutes and Stansted £10 for 15 minutes. The RAC found the average fee rose to £7, up £1.70, while London City Airport’s £8 for five minutes makes it the priciest per minute. Airports UK says free drop-offs remain available, framing fees as part of business and climate strategies. The trend contrasts with Europe’s top 10 airports, where none charge for drop-offs.

crisis.title: Global Oil Prices Rise as Conflict With Iran Deepens
crisis.summary: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

breaking.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
breaking.summary: Google’s parent company Alphabet reported negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion in its latest quarter, the first time in over a decade, as AI infrastructure costs surged to $45 billion in Q2 alone. Total AI spending is projected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion, driven by server and data center investments. Despite revenue hitting $119.8 billion, a 23% year-over-year increase, Alphabet’s stock dropped 4% in after-hours trading. The company’s CFO attributed the cash burn to capital expenditures tied entirely to AI expansion.

breaking.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
breaking.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

breaking.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
breaking.summary: The Trump administration announced an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

sports.title: HNK Rijeka tie 'a great opportunity' for Derry
sports.summary: Derry City face HNK Rijeka in the second round of the UEFA Conference League qualifiers on August 1. The tie follows Derry’s 5-3 aggregate loss to CSKA Sofia in the Europa League opener, where crowd trouble at Brandywell led to a temporary match stoppage and police intervention. As a result, Derry received a suspended partial stadium ban for two years and a €20,000 fine. Lynch’s side aims to recover from the setback with a strong home performance.

sports.title: Weekly sports quiz: Glasgow 2026, The Open and The Hundred
sports.summary: Glasgow will host the 2026 Commonwealth Games, with preparations underway as record transfer spending shakes up football and The Hundred cricket tournament kicks off this summer. Last week’s sports quiz saw only 6% of participants score full marks. The event follows a busy week in sports, including the World Cup final build-up. Stay updated on quizzes and sports news via the dedicated pages.

sports.title: Ex-UFC champion Andrade working as delivery driver
sports.summary: Jessica Andrade, former UFC strawweight champion, is working as a delivery driver in Brazil to earn extra income while recovering from a shoulder injury. The 34-year-old, who hasn’t competed since August 2023, has fought 30 times in the UFC since 2013. She also started a landscaping project at a friend’s house to supplement her earnings. Andrade plans to return to fighting once fully recovered.

facts.title: River levels approach all-time low as drought announcement looms
facts.summary: River levels in Wales are nearing record lows as drought conditions intensify. Natural Resources Wales is set to declare an environmental drought for parts of the country on Thursday, following a July with just 5% of average rainfall. The dry spell, worsened by back-to-back heatwaves, has devastated wildlife and threatened crops and livestock. Farmers and conservation groups warn of worsening impacts if conditions persist.

facts.title: Butterfly city blooms in shadow of office blocks
facts.summary: A mini meadow in Hull city centre has attracted ten butterfly species, including the rare small skipper, thriving amid office blocks and a shopping centre. The 2023 project, led by Hull Butterfly City, allows grass and wildflowers to grow on a Land Registry site near St Stephen’s Shopping Centre to support caterpillars. Sean Clough noted the small skipper’s winter survival depends on uncut grass. The scheme aims to boost urban wildlife by providing specific host plants for caterpillars.

facts.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
facts.summary: A stunning NASA image from July 23, 2026 captures the rugged Olympic Mountains in Washington state, showcasing snow-capped peaks and deep river valleys. The astronaut-taken panorama highlights the peninsula’s mix of alpine glaciers, temperate rainforests, and coastal wilderness. Olympic National Park covers much of the mountainous core, with surrounding areas managed as national forest, state land, or tribal territory. The mountains’ basalt rock formed 55 to 15 million years ago from undersea volcanic activity.

tech.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
tech.summary: ServiceNow has invested $40 million in Indian banking software specialist BusinessNext, valuing the 24-year-old firm at $700 million and taking a roughly 5% stake. This deal gives BusinessNext access to ServiceNow's global sales network as they expand their partnership in AI for financial services. BusinessNext serves over 70 banks across India, Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and the US, with half of its revenue coming from outside India. The partnership is expected to drive growth for BusinessNext in overseas markets.

tech.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
tech.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine has been created, priced at $200, featuring a basic fully functional game with a working joystick and button to control Mario's movement and jumping. The machine includes an endless series of barrels for Mario to jump, utilizing the same original barrels first released in 1989 for Lego's pirate sets. Shigeru Miyamoto, creator of Mario, has reportedly expressed approval for the set. The machine's design is a nostalgic tribute to classic arcade games.

tech.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
tech.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

health.title: Alarm over hospital lung admissions for children in England’s poorest postcodes
health.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalized for respiratory infections than those in wealthier regions, new NHS data shows. In 2024/25, 1,941 per 100,000 children in the most deprived areas were admitted, compared to 1,380 per 100,000 in the least deprived. Experts link the disparity to factors like air pollution, though the data doesn’t specify causes. Health officials plan further research to address the growing concern.

health.title: Life-changing MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
health.summary: A new NHS England scheme will provide the MS drug fampridine to up to 5,000 patients annually, improving walking ability for those with multiple sclerosis. The treatment, which boosts nerve signals, showed mobility gains in 40% of trial participants. Previously costly, it was already available in other UK regions. Patients will now access it alongside existing NHS support like physiotherapy.

entertainment.title: Fatboy Slim helps to save 'lifeblood' music venue
entertainment.summary: A music venue charity has bought its tenth UK club to protect grassroots music, making it the country’s largest dedicated landlord of live music spaces. The Pipeline in Brighton was saved from closure after years of financial pressure, securing 6,000 jobs and reversing a 13% rise in grassroots gig attendance lost last year. The charity’s model buys venues to keep rents affordable for artists and communities. Next, it plans to expand its network to prevent further closures.

entertainment.title: Courtney Stodden Is Fighting for Something Bigger Than Jason Alexander’s Apology
entertainment.summary: In 2012, Jason Alexander apologized to Courtney Stodden for a 2012 "Dr. Phil" parody sketch where he made inappropriate comments and simulated fondling her when she was 17. The sketch, part of "The Donny Clay Show," was removed from Funny or Die and YouTube. Stodden, who married actor Doug Hutchison at 16, is now seeking accountability after years of public scrutiny. Alexander’s apology follows years of criticism over the sketch’s exploitation of a minor.

entertainment.title: ‘The Wild Robot’ VFX Studio STIM Sets Canary Islands Operation, Planning to Create More Than 100 Jobs
entertainment.summary: French animation and VFX studio STIM is launching a new production center in Spain’s Canary Islands, aiming to create over 100 jobs by 2026. Operations at STIM Tenerife begin in August, with 60 roles already open for skilled artists and supervisors. The studio chose the Canary Islands for its low-tax ZEC regime, tapping into a growing creative talent pool. This expansion will boost STIM’s production capacity while maintaining its high artistic and technical standards.
